description: Someone chooses to join a repair day, then commits to return each morning until the roof is secure. 'seru' is a self-binding undertaking to follow a stated course beyond the moment of selection. 'kanu' (choose) names the selection, 'naseru' (obligation) attributes a requirement from some source, and 'korei' (fulfill) reports that what was undertaken has been carried out.
articulatory_notes: Keep the tongue blade close to the alveolar ridge for unvoiced 's' and release the air into stressed true-mid front unrounded 'e'. Make a tap or short trill with the tongue tip for 'r', then raise the back of the tongue and round the lips for high-back 'u'.
usage_notes: A practice, project, or course can be the direct object of 'seru'; a complete action being undertaken takes the 'mena ... meno' frame. Noun 'seru' is a commitment or the act of committing. Its scope, conditions, and duration matter especially when others may rely on it.

examples.phi: mia mena mia so turema meno seru.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: A practice may depend on commitments renewed after distraction or failure. The word names the undertaking without pretending that resolve makes performance automatic.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A speaker can commit to returning to a difficult conversation while leaving the other person's participation and boundaries separate.

semantic_domains.activity: undertaking to follow a stated course of action
semantic_domains.community: a commitment on which another person or group may rely
semantic_domains.temporal: an undertaking meant to continue beyond the moment it is made
